classification.reasoning: The patch involves structural changes in the Java source code, including adding new fields and methods, modifying existing methods to handle new parameters, and changing the behavior of classes related to resize operations and garbage collection of dangling indices. It also includes additions to version handling and test cases. The changes are not simple string replacements or whitespace fixes, but involve understanding and adapting to API changes and internal logic, which is characteristic of TYPE_III complexity. The localization evidence shows a mix of exact and pickaxe matches, supporting a moderate to high confidence in this classification. The token budget estimate is moderate due to the complexity and size of the patch, requiring careful backporting and testing.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of ResizeResponse and Metadata classes to support new logic involving resize responses and cluster metadata.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable handling of resize responses and access to cluster metadata for enhanced resize operations.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Introduced logic to detect and identify stale indices related to the source index by scanning cluster metadata for indices whose resize source UUID matches the source index UUID.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: AlterTableClient, method handling resize operation preparation
hunk_descriptions.intent: To find any existing stale resized indices that need to be cleaned up before performing a new resize operation.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Changed resize execution to conditionally perform garbage collection of stale dangling artifacts before executing the resize request if a stale index UUID was found.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure cleanup of stale indices related to previous resize operations before starting a new resize, preventing conflicts or resource leaks.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Removed the deleteTempIndices method and its invocation, effectively eliminating a prior step that deleted temporary indices before resize.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: AlterTableClient class, deleteTempIndices method and its usage
hunk_descriptions.intent: To simplify or change the cleanup strategy by removing the generic deletion of temporary indices, possibly replaced by more targeted cleanup.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Refactored GCDanglingArtifactsRequest to support specifying a list of index UUIDs to delete; added serialization logic for this list and a static instance representing deletion of all dangling indices.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: GCDanglingArtifactsRequest class, constructors and serialization methods
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable targeted garbage collection of dangling indices by UUIDs, improving control over cleanup operations.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Enhanced the logic to delete dangling indices either for all dangling indices when no UUIDs are specified or selectively for given index UUIDs provided in the request.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: TransportGCDanglingArtifacts class, method handling dangling artifacts deletion
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support selective garbage collection of dangling indices by UUID, allowing more precise cleanup operations.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Changed the execution of the garbage collection action to use the new static ALL instance of GCDanglingArtifactsRequest instead of the previous INSTANCE.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To align with the updated GCDanglingArtifactsRequest API that distinguishes between all and selective deletion requests.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added new version constants V_6_0_4 and V_6_1_1 to the Version class.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To introduce new version identifiers for compatibility and feature gating in the codebase.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of java.util.List to support new logic involving lists in the TransportResize class.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable usage of List collections in the class, likely for handling multiple index UUIDs or related data.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Modified the error handling path after a failed resize acknowledgment to perform targeted garbage collection of the resized index UUID instead of a generic cleanup; adjusted lambda parameters for clarity.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: TransportResize class, method handling resize response completion
hunk_descriptions.intent: To clean up the specific resized index on failure, preventing dangling indices and improving error recovery.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of ResizeResponse and Metadata classes to support new logic related to resize responses and cluster metadata.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable handling of resize responses and access to cluster metadata for enhanced resize operations.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Introduced logic to detect stale indices related to the source index by scanning cluster metadata for indices whose resize source UUID matches the source index UUID.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: AlterTableClient, method handling resize operation preparation
hunk_descriptions.intent: To identify and track any stale indices that were previously created from the source index to clean them up before resizing again.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Changed the resize execution flow to conditionally run a garbage collection of dangling artifacts for the detected stale index UUID before performing the resize operation.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: AlterTableClient, method executing the resize request
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that any stale indices related to previous resize operations are cleaned up before starting a new resize, preventing conflicts or resource leaks.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Removed the deleteTempIndices method and its invocation, effectively eliminating a prior step that deleted temporary indices before resizing.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To simplify or change the cleanup strategy by removing the blanket deletion of temporary indices, possibly replaced by more targeted cleanup logic.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Refactored GCDanglingArtifactsRequest to support specifying a list of index UUIDs to delete, with an empty list indicating deletion of all dangling indices; added serialization logic for the new field and a static instance representing all indices.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable targeted garbage collection of dangling indices by UUID, improving control over which indices are cleaned up during GC operations.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Enhanced the logic to delete dangling indices either for all indices if no UUIDs are specified or selectively for provided index UUIDs, checking metadata for each UUID to identify dangling indices.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support selective garbage collection of dangling indices by UUID, allowing more precise cleanup operations instead of always deleting all dangling indices.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced usage of the singleton GCDanglingArtifactsRequest.INSTANCE with the new GCDanglingArtifactsRequest.ALL static instance when executing the GC action.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To align with the updated GCDanglingArtifactsRequest API that uses ALL to represent deletion of all dangling indices, improving clarity and consistency.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added new version constants V_6_0_4 and V_6_1_1 to the Version class, updating the versioning scheme.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To introduce new version identifiers for compatibility and feature gating in the system.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of java.util.List to support new code additions involving lists.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable usage of List collections in the TransportResize class.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Modified the error handling path after a failed resize acknowledgment to perform targeted garbage collection of the resized index UUID instead of a blanket GC; also adjusted lambda parameter names for clarity.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To clean up only the specific resized index when resize fails, avoiding unnecessary deletion of unrelated dangling indices and improving error recovery.
